expectations.text: Is European Portuguese: uses 'queria' (and/or 'era') as the polite request form, NOT 'gostaria' (BP) or 'quereria' (textbook EP nobody uses)
expectations.evidence: Dialogue uses 'Queria uma bica e um pastel de nata, se faz favor' — correct EP polite request

expectations.text: No BP forms slip in: no você (as the subject pronoun), no gerúndio for ongoing action (estar a + inf is EP)
expectations.evidence: Uses tu address ('encontras', 'podes', 'lembra-te', 'troca'); no você as subject; no gerúndio

expectations.text: All non-cognate Portuguese words are translated to English
expectations.evidence: Vocabulary table has translations, but the dialogue lines themselves (Bom dia! Faz favor? / Com certeza. É para tomar aqui ou para levar? / São dois euros e vinte. / Aqui tem. Obrigado!) have NO line-by-line English glosses. Learner sees raw Portuguese without translation in the dialogue.

expectations.text: Uses 'Estudante' / 'Data' headers (worksheet conventions per project), or at least Portuguese cognate headers (not 'Student' / 'Date')
expectations.evidence: No student-info header block at top. Jumps from title straight into Introdução. Missing the Estudante/Data convention.

expectations.text: No fake accents on Portuguese words (real spellings only)
Generic learner audience — no specific roster student name (Marvin, Christian, Amanda, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Dialogue speakers labeled 'Empregado' and 'Cliente' — no proper names
Dialogue has 6 lines (3 from each speaker)

expectations.text: Lisbon-specific vocabulary present (bica, galão, pastel de nata, se faz favor) and explained
expectations.evidence: Vocabulary table includes bica, meia de leite, galão, pastel de nata, se faz favor with English translations
